manual_ambiguous_groups.site: Welcome Break Warwick Services Southbound, M40
manual_ambiguous_groups.reason_for_operator: Two separate brands share the service-station forecourt. Google Places returns one listing for the whole site; the FSA records each brand separately. Until each brand has a distinct Google Business Profile, both venues are flagged ambiguous and remain in Directional-C.
manual_ambiguous_groups.resolution_path: Each brand operator requests a distinct Google Business Profile for their unit (franchise head office typically handles this for chain brands).
manual_ambiguous_groups.note: Two FSA records attached to the same Google Place (same service station unit). Mark both ambiguous.

manual_ambiguous_groups.reason_for_operator: Two FSA records at different postcodes share one Google Place. Most commonly this is a rebrand or a relocation where the Google Business Profile survived and the old FSA record was not retired. Without operator confirmation it is not safe to assume either is closed.
manual_ambiguous_groups.resolution_path: Operator confirms whether 'The Margin' or 'The Water Margin @ The Hollybush' is the current trading entity. Once confirmed, set `fsa_closed = true` on the retired record (via a manual patch on the establishments file) and remove the retired FHRSID from this entry.
manual_ambiguous_groups.note: Two FSA records pointing at the same Google Place at different postcodes. Likely a rebrand or a Google matching error; mark both ambiguous until disambiguated.

manual_ambiguous_groups.site: Jaguar Land Rover, Gaydon Test Centre, Banbury Road, Gaydon
manual_ambiguous_groups.reason_for_operator: Three separately FSA-registered food units inside a single private industrial site (JLR Gaydon Test Centre). Google Places has one listing for the site as a whole, so all three FHRS records resolve to the same Google Place ID. Public ranking is not the right surface for private-site canteens; Directional-C is the correct permanent class here.
manual_ambiguous_groups.resolution_path: These records will likely remain Directional-C permanently. If distinct Google listings exist for each unit, the alias table can be updated per unit; otherwise accept the Directional-C classification as the honest output.
manual_ambiguous_groups.note: Three FSA records at a single MoD / industrial site sharing one Google Place. All ambiguous; Directional-C is the correct permanent state.

manual_ambiguous_groups.reason_for_operator: Two separately FSA-registered units inside the Bell Court food hall share one Google Place ID (likely the hall's shared listing). Until each unit has a distinct Google Business Profile, both remain ambiguous and land in Directional-C.
manual_ambiguous_groups.resolution_path: Each Bell Court unit requests a distinct Google Business Profile. Operators of multi-tenant food halls can do this via Google Business Profile Help.
manual_ambiguous_groups.note: Two Bell Court units sharing one Google Place. Ambiguous.

known_unresolved_not_in_slice.note: High-profile venue. Not in the 210-record trial FSA slice. Consumer search must not silently return nothing; surface as 'not yet in trial dataset' with a link to feedback.
known_unresolved_not_in_slice.unblock: Re-run .github/scripts/augment_fsa_stratford.py to pull all food business types for LA 320, or expand the trial slice.

known_unresolved_not_in_slice.note: Operated by RSC Enterprises Ltd. Not in trial slice.
No FSA record plausibly maps to an 'Oscar's' trading name in Stratford. Either closed, never FSA-registered, or outside trial scope. Consumer search should return 'unknown venue'.
known_unresolved_not_in_slice.unblock: Confirm existence via Google / TripAdvisor lookup; if still operational, add a manual FHRSID + expansion.
